input.topic: Is GPT-4o-mini suitable for production use?

sources.title: OpenAI announcement
sources.content: GPT-4o-mini offers 60% of GPT-4o quality at 6% of the cost, suitable for many production workloads.

sources.title: Anthropic benchmarks
sources.content: Independent testing shows GPT-4o-mini underperforms Claude Haiku on reasoning tasks but matches it on cost.

expected.executive_summary: GPT-4o-mini is broadly suitable for production at a significant cost advantage over GPT-4o [1], though independent benchmarks suggest it trails Claude Haiku on reasoning while matching its price [2]. The choice depends on the workload — cost-sensitive non-reasoning tasks favor GPT-4o-mini.

key_points.claim: GPT-4o-mini offers 60% of GPT-4o quality at 6% of the cost.
GPT-4o-mini underperforms Claude Haiku on reasoning tasks.
GPT-4o-mini matches Claude Haiku on cost.

disagreements: Source 1 frames GPT-4o-mini as broadly production-ready; source 2 calls out a reasoning gap vs Claude Haiku.
open_questions: What specific reasoning benchmarks did source 2 use?
Does the quality gap matter for our actual workload mix?
